entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Baseline Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture
entries.summary: Learn how to build network-secured, highly available, and zone-redundant enterprise chat applications by using Microsoft Foundry tools and Azure App Service.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Baseline Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture in an Azure Landing Zone
entries.summary: Learn about deploying the baseline Microsoft Foundry, Foundry Agent Service, Azure OpenAI in Foundry Models, and Azure App Service chat architecture in an Azure landing zone.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Develop a RAG Solution - Chunking Phase
entries.summary: Learn about the various chunking strategies, like boundary based, custom code, and document analysis models.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Develop a RAG Solution - Chunk Enrichment Phase
entries.summary: Learn how to augment your chunks with metadata like title, summary, keywords, and entities, and learn common cleaning methods that can improve vector matches.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Develop a RAG Solution - Generate Embeddings Phase
entries.summary: Learn how embeddings work, how to choose an embedding model, and how your embedding model can affect your vector search results.

entries.title: [In preview] Public Preview: New project templates and template gallery for Azure Functions in VS Code

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Rate Limiting Pattern
entries.summary: Control the rate at which your application sends requests to a service so that you stay within the service's throttling limits and overall capacity.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: SD-WAN Integration with Azure Hub-and-Spoke Network Topologies
entries.summary: Learn how to integrate SD-WAN with Azure hub-and-spoke networks by using Route Server, ExpressRoute, and network virtual appliances (NVAs) for hybrid connectivity.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Queue-Based Load Leveling Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to smooth intermittent heavy loads by using a queue that acts as a buffer between a task and the service that it invokes.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Build a Multiple-Agent Workflow Automation Solution by using Microsoft Agent Framework
entries.summary: Learn how to design and deploy scalable multi‑agent automation infrastructure by using Microsoft Agent Framework, Azure Container Apps, and Foundry Tools.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Real-Time Analytics with Azure Service Bus and Microsoft Fabric
entries.summary: Learn how to use Microsoft Fabric with Azure Service Bus for near real-time solutions at enterprise scale in production environments.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Extract text from objects using Power Automate and AI Builder
entries.summary: Use AI Builder and Azure Document Intelligence in Foundry Tools in a Power Automate workflow to extract text from images, for indexing and retrieval.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Computer Forensics Chain of Custody in Azure
entries.summary: Learn how to create an infrastructure and workflow to help ensure a valid digital evidence chain of custody (CoC) for computer forensics in Azure.

entries.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud are transitioning to a Microsoft Agent 365 license

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Automate Document Classification in Azure
entries.summary: Learn how to use the durable functions feature of Azure Functions as an automated document processing pipeline to classify documents by type.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Gatekeeper Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to use the Gatekeeper pattern to protect applications and services by using a dedicated host instance as a broker to validate requests and data.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Gateway Aggregation Pattern
entries.summary: Learn about the Gateway Aggregation pattern, which uses a gateway to aggregate many individual requests into a single request.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Strangler Fig Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to incrementally migrate a legacy system by gradually replacing specific pieces of functionality with new applications and services.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Integrate IBM mainframe and midrange message queues with Azure
entries.summary: This example describes a data-first approach to middleware integration that enables IBM message queues (MQs).

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Basic Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture
entries.summary: Establish foundational knowledge for how to build and deploy enterprise chat applications by using Microsoft Foundry and Azure App Service.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Compare AWS and Azure Compute Services
entries.summary: Compare the compute services in Azure and Amazon Web Services (AWS). Explore the differences in virtual machines (VMs), containers, and serverless technologies.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Anti-Corruption Layer Pattern
entries.summary: Learn about the Anti-Corruption Layer pattern. Implement a facade or adapter layer between a modern application and a legacy system.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Throttling Pattern
entries.summary: Control the resources that an application instance, tenant, or service consumes so that the system continues to meet service-level objectives (SLOs) under load.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Data partitioning strategies
entries.summary: View guidance on separating data partitions to be managed and accessed separately in different services, such as Azure AI Search, Azure storage queues, and more.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Dynamic AI Agents at Scale Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to dynamically select and orchestrate AI agents from a pool by using Microsoft Foundry, Azure AI Search, and Azure OpenAI to build multiagent systems.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: CI/CD for Microservices
entries.summary: Learn about continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) for microservices, including challenges and recommended approaches.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Connect an On-Premises Network to Azure by Using ExpressRoute with VPN Failover
entries.summary: Learn how to connect an on-premises network to an Azure virtual network by using ExpressRoute with a virtual private network (VPN) gateway failover.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Use a Gateway in Front of Multiple Azure OpenAI Deployments or Instances
entries.summary: Learn how to add a gateway in front of multiple Azure OpenAI model deployments or instances.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Automate API Deployments by Using APIOps
entries.summary: Use APIOps and Azure API Management to build and deploy APIs. This solution provides self-service tools, auditing, policy enforcement, and early feedback.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Baseline Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture
entries.summary: Learn how to build network-secured, highly available, and zone-redundant enterprise chat applications by using Microsoft Foundry tools and Azure App Service.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Basic Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture
entries.summary: Establish foundational knowledge for how to build and deploy enterprise chat applications by using Microsoft Foundry and Azure App Service.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: AI Agent Orchestration Patterns
entries.summary: Learn about fundamental orchestration patterns for AI agent architectures, including sequential, concurrent, group chat, handoff, and magentic patterns.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: WordPress on App Service
entries.summary: Learn how to use Azure Front Door, Azure App Service, and other Azure services to deploy a highly scalable and secure installation of WordPress.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Quantum computing integration with classical apps
entries.summary: Learn how to use Azure Quantum to implement hybrid quantum applications

entries.title: New architecture guidance: External Configuration Store Pattern
entries.summary: Review the External Configuration Store pattern, which moves configuration information out of the application deployment package to a centralized location.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Image classification on Azure
entries.summary: Learn how to build image classification into your applications by using Azure services such as Azure AI Content Understanding and Azure Functions.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Access Foundry Models and Other Language Models Through a Gateway
entries.summary: Understand how implementing a gateway helps manage access to Azure OpenAI and other language models more securely and efficiently.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Extract and Analyze Call Center Data by Using Azure OpenAI, Speech Services, and Language Services
entries.summary: Learn how to extract insights from customer conversations at a call center by using Foundry Tools and Azure OpenAI.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Deployment Stamps Pattern
entries.summary: Learn about the Deployment Stamps pattern, which deploys many independent copies (known as stamps, service units, or scale units) of application components.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Use a Split-Brain DNS Configuration to Host a Web App
entries.summary: Implement a split-brain DNS configuration to differentiate traffic treatment based on DNS and whether a client originates from the internet or a corporate network.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Choose a Compute Option for Microservices
entries.summary: Choose an Azure compute platform for a microservices architecture. Compare options based on interservice communication, independent scaling, and deployability.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Certificate Life Cycle Management on Azure
entries.summary: Learn how to create an infrastructure and workflow to automate the renewal process for certificates issued by a nonintegrated certification authority (CA).

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Azure Load Testing with Custom Plugins for Event Hubs and IoT Hub to Simulate Device Behaviors
entries.summary: Learn how to design KPIs and develop a dashboard for Azure Load Testing with custom JMeter plugins to simulate device behaviors.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Compensating Transaction Pattern
entries.summary: Use the Compensating Transaction pattern to undo work when a step of an eventually consistent operation fails in distributed systems.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Choose a Microsoft Fabric Deployment Pattern
entries.summary: Evaluate four Microsoft Fabric deployment patterns to structure capacities, workspaces, and items based on your requirements.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Event Sourcing Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to use an append-only store to record the full series of events that describe actions taken on data in a domain.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Basic Enterprise Integration on Azure
entries.summary: Learn how to implement a basic enterprise integration architecture on Azure by using Azure Logic Apps and Azure API Management.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Asynchronous Request-Reply Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to decouple back-end processing from front-end hosts by using asynchronous operations and HTTP polling for long-running tasks.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Computer Forensics Chain of Custody in Azure
entries.summary: Learn how to create an infrastructure and workflow to help ensure a valid digital evidence chain of custody (CoC) for computer forensics in Azure.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Baseline Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture
entries.summary: Learn how to build network-secured, highly available, and zone-redundant enterprise chat applications by using Microsoft Foundry tools and Azure App Service.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Basic Microsoft Foundry Chat Reference Architecture
entries.summary: Establish foundational knowledge for how to build and deploy enterprise chat applications by using Microsoft Foundry and Azure App Service.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Cross-tenant Secure Access to Apps by Using Private Endpoints
entries.summary: Restrict inbound traffic to a web app or function app. Use private endpoints in Azure to give consumer tenants secure access to provider tenant apps.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Competing Consumers Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how the Competing Consumers pattern supports multiple concurrent consumers processing messages received on the same messaging channel.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Multitier Web Application Built for High Availability and Disaster Recovery
entries.summary: Learn how to create a resilient multitier web application built for high availability and disaster recovery on Azure by using VMs.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Sharding Pattern
entries.summary: Use the Sharding design pattern to divide a data store into horizontal partitions or shards to improve scalability and performance in distributed systems.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Compute Resource Consolidation Pattern
entries.summary: Use the Compute Resource Consolidation design pattern to consolidate multiple tasks or operations into one computational unit.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Choreography Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to set up services to decide when and how to process a business operation, instead of depending on a central orchestrator.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Real-Time Analytics with Azure Service Bus and Microsoft Fabric
entries.summary: Learn how to use Microsoft Fabric with Azure Service Bus for near real-time solutions at enterprise scale in production environments.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Migrate a Web App by Using Azure API Management
entries.summary: Review an example scenario in which an e-commerce company in the travel industry uses Azure API Management to migrate a legacy web application.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Design Patterns for Microservices
entries.summary: Learn about design patterns that address common microservices challenges like data consistency, cross-service communication, failure isolation, and legacy integration.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Best Practices for Background Jobs
entries.summary: Learn best practices for designing background jobs on Azure, including hosting options, reliability, and scaling for event-driven and scheduled jobs.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Event Sourcing Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to use an append-only store to record the full series of events that describe actions taken on data in a domain.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: High-volume batch transaction processing
entries.summary: Use Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and Azure Service Bus to implement high-volume batch transaction processing.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Ambassador Pattern
entries.summary: Learn about the ambassador pattern, which creates helper services that send network requests on behalf of a consumer service or application.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Bulkhead Pattern
entries.summary: Learn about the Bulkhead pattern, which isolates elements of an application into pools so that if one fails, the others continue to function.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Get Started with Compute Architecture Design
entries.summary: Learn about Azure compute technologies, from virtual machines to serverless functions. Review guidance, solution ideas, and reference architectures.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Multiregion Load Balancing
entries.summary: Learn how to build an Azure system that serves web and non-web workloads with resilient multitier applications in multiple Azure regions.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Event-Driven Architecture Style
entries.summary: Explore the benefits, challenges, and best practices for event-driven architectures on Azure.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Asynchronous Request-Reply Pattern
entries.summary: Learn how to decouple back-end processing from front-end hosts by using asynchronous operations and HTTP polling for long-running tasks.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Publisher-Subscriber Pattern
entries.summary: Use an intermediary message broker or event bus to decouple senders from consumers and distribute messages to interested subscribers asynchronously.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Real-Time Analytics with Azure Service Bus and Microsoft Fabric
entries.summary: Learn how to use Microsoft Fabric with Azure Service Bus for near real-time solutions at enterprise scale in production environments.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Data partitioning strategies
entries.summary: View guidance on separating data partitions to be managed and accessed separately in different services, such as Azure AI Search, Azure storage queues, and more.

entries.title: New architecture guidance: Host Name Preservation
entries.summary: Learn how to preserve the original HTTP host name between a reverse proxy and its back-end web application in common Azure services.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Azure API Management Landing Zone Architecture
entries.summary: Learn about a secure enterprise API management architecture that uses Azure Application Gateway, API Management, and CI/CD pipeline deployment.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Enhanced-security hybrid messaging for client access
entries.summary: Enhance your security in a client access scenario by using Microsoft Entra multifactor authentication.

entries.title: Updated architecture guidance: Enhanced-security hybrid messaging for mobile access
entries.summary: Enhance your security in a mobile access scenario by using Microsoft Entra multifactor authentication.

entries.summary: Publish existing APIM-managed APIs as Model Context Protocol tools so AI agents (Foundry, GitHub Copilot, any MCP client) consume governed tools with gateway policies in front.

entries.title: Logic Apps agent workflows (agent loop) in preview
entries.summary: Build AI agents inside Logic Apps Standard: an agent loop action reasons with Azure OpenAI models and invokes any of 1,400+ connectors as tools, with full run history.
